title: Sri Devaadi Raja Perumal Temple
tagline: Sri Devaadi Raja Perumal Temple — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

heroImage.alt: Sri Devaadi Raja Perumal Temple, Tamil Nadu

overview.short: The Devaadi Raja Perumal temple (Tamil: தேவாதிராஜன் பெருமாள் கோயில்) (also called Amaruviappan temple) in Therazhundur, a village in Mayiladuthurai district of the South Indian sta
overview.description: The Devaadi Raja Perumal temple (Tamil: தேவாதிராஜன் பெருமாள் கோயில்) (also called Amaruviappan temple) in Therazhundur, a village in Mayiladuthurai district of the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu, is dedicated to the Hindu god Vishnu. Constructed in the Dravidian style of architecture, the temple is glorified in the Naalayira Divya Prabandham, the early medieval Tamil canon of the Alvar saints from the 6th–9th centuries CE. It is one of the 108 Divya Desams dedicated to Vishnu, who is worshipped as Amaruviappan, and his consort Lakshmi as Sengamalavalli Thayar. The original structure of the t

topPlaces.name: Kachirayanatham
topPlaces.description: Kachirayanatham is a village in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. This village was originally ruled by a king called 'Kachirayan' leading to the naming of the village as Kachirayanatham.

topPlaces.name: Mangalampet
topPlaces.description: Mangalampet is a panchayat town in Cuddalore district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu.

topPlaces.name: Mepuliyur
topPlaces.description: Meppuliyur is a village in Ulundurpet Taluk, Kallakurichi District, Tamil Nadu, India. The rural population depends on agriculture.

topPlaces.name: Veerattikuppam
topPlaces.description: Veerattikuppam is a village in Virudhachalam taluk, Cuddalore district, Tamil Nadu, India. It is located 12 kilometres (7.5 mi) from Virudhachalam and 5 kilometres (3.1 mi) from Neyveli.

topPlaces.name: Konankuppam
topPlaces.description: Konankuppam is a small village situated in the Cuddalore district of Tamil Nadu in India. It is the place where Rev.

topPlaces.name: Kolanjiappar temple
topPlaces.description: Kolanjiappar temple in Manavalanallur, a village on the outskirts of Virudhachalam in Cuddalore district in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu, is dedicated to the Hindu god Murugan. Constructed in the Dravidian architecture, the temple is located in the Virudhachalam - Manavalanallur Road.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Sri Devaadi Raja Perumal Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Sri Devaadi Raja Perumal Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Sri Devaadi Raja Perumal Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Tiruchirappalli International Airport (115 km) and the nearest railway station is Tiruchirappalli Junction (113 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Sri Devaadi Raja Perumal Temple?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Sri Devaadi Raja Perumal Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Sri Devaadi Raja Perumal Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Sri Devaadi Raja Perumal Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Sri Devaadi Raja Perumal Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kachirayanatham, Mangalampet, Mepuliyur, Veerattikuppam, Konankuppam.
